reasoning: France is the clear market favorite after reaching the quarterfinals without conceding a goal, with a deep, star-laden squad and a proven finalist pedigree. Its next test is Morocco, a genuine banana-skin, but the model and market both rate Les Bleus highest. Spain surged to second after eliminating Portugal, carrying elite young talent in Yamal and Pedri, while defending champions Argentina and a resurgent England (fresh off beating Mexico at altitude in Azteca) are the main threats. My dark horse is Norway, who stunned Brazil in the Round of 16 and ride Erling Haaland's goalscoring form with everything to gain and no pressure, though they face England next. The wide-open bracket and knockout volatility keep confidence at Medium.
